title: Savely Store MS Data Objects in a Portable Stash
text: Data objects R can serialized disk R’s rds RData format using base R save() function re-imported using load() function. R-specific binary data format can however used easily programming languages preventing exchange R data objects software programming languages. MsStash package defines basic classes generic methods export import mass spectrometry (MS) data objects various storage formats aiming facilitate data exchange software. SpectraStash package implements portable data storage formats (stashes) data classes Spectra package, including Spectra object ’s various data backends.

title: Savely Store MS Data Objects in a Portable Stash
text: package can installed BiocManager package. install BiocManager use install.packages("BiocManager") , , BiocManager::install("RforMassSpectrometry/SpectraStash") install package.

title: Savely Store MS Data Objects in a Portable Stash
text: MS data objects can saved restored saveMsObject() readMsObject() functions () MS data stashes. Supported stash formats respective parameter objects : PlainTextParam: storage data (custom) plain text file format. AlabasterParam: storage MS data using Bioconductor’s r Biocpkg("alabaster.base") framework using files HDF5 JSON format. MS stashes format fully support functions saveObject() readObject() alabaster.base. See also vignette MsStash details formats implementation notes. example create Spectra object two example MS data files MsDataHub package. next filter data restricting spectra mass peaks retention time 20 200 seconds m/z 110 120. next store Spectra object SpectraStash using saveMsObject() function. use alabaster format define location stash path parameter AlabasterParam. present example save temporary folder. content stash folder : alabaster format, slot Spectra object stored sub directory. Spectra objects don’t handle MS data , rely MsBackend provide data. MsBackend used Spectra object stored ’s stash located backend directory SpectraStash. Spectra object can restored readMsObject(): need specify type object restore first parameter function - case Spectra(). full Spectra object restored, including processing queue history. can also read (restore) MsBackend SpectraStash. Since present stash alabaster format can either use readMsObject() also readObject() alabaster.base: using readMsObject():
code: library(Spectra) library(SpectraStash) library(MsDataHub) fls <- c(X20171016_POOL_POS_1_105.134.mzML(),          X20171016_POOL_POS_3_105.134.mzML()) sps <- Spectra(fls) sps ## MSn data (Spectra) with 1862 spectra in a MsBackendMzR backend: ##        msLevel     rtime scanIndex ##      <integer> <numeric> <integer> ## 1            1     0.280         1 ## 2            1     0.559         2 ## 3            1     0.838         3 ## 4            1     1.117         4 ## 5            1     1.396         5 ## ...        ...       ...       ... ## 1858         1   258.636       927 ## 1859         1   258.915       928 ## 1860         1   259.194       929 ## 1861         1   259.473       930 ## 1862         1   259.752       931 ##  ... 34 more variables/columns. ##  ## file(s): ## 5e1225b6b7_7859 ## 5e149b7c978_7860 sps <- filterRt(sps, c(20, 200)) sps <- filterMzRange(sps, c(110, 120)) sps ## MSn data (Spectra) with 1290 spectra in a MsBackendMzR backend: ##        msLevel     rtime scanIndex ##      <integer> <numeric> <integer> ## 1            1    20.089        72 ## 2            1    20.368        73 ## 3            1    20.647        74 ## 4            1    20.926        75 ## 5            1    21.205        76 ## ...        ...       ...       ... ## 1286         1   198.649       712 ## 1287         1   198.928       713 ## 1288         1   199.207       714 ## 1289         1   199.486       715 ## 1290         1   199.765       716 ##  ... 34 more variables/columns. ##  ## file(s): ## 5e1225b6b7_7859 ## 5e149b7c978_7860 ## Lazy evaluation queue: 1 processing step(s) ## Processing: ##  Filter: select retention time [20..200] on MS level(s)  [Thu Jun 25 13:55:13 2026] ##  Filter: select peaks with an m/z within [110, 120] [Thu Jun 25 13:55:13 2026] #' Define the location of the stash d <- file.path(tempfile(), "spectra_stash")  #' Configure the format and location ap <- AlabasterParam(d)  #' Save the `Spectra` object to the stash saveMsObject(sps, ap) library(fs) dir_tree(d) ## /tmp/RtmpYSFV3h/file7f52a658644/spectra_stash ## ├── OBJECT ## ├── _environment.json ## ├── backend ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   └── spectra_data ## │       ├── OBJECT ## │       └── basic_columns.h5 ## ├── metadata ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   └── list_contents.json.gz ## ├── processing ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   └── contents.h5 ## ├── processing_chunk_size ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   └── contents.h5 ## ├── processing_queue_variables ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   └── contents.h5 ## └── spectra_processing_queue.json res <- readMsObject(Spectra(), ap) res ## MSn data (Spectra) with 1290 spectra in a MsBackendMzR backend: ##        msLevel     rtime scanIndex ##      <integer> <numeric> <integer> ## 1            1    20.089        72 ## 2            1    20.368        73 ## 3            1    20.647        74 ## 4            1    20.926        75 ## 5            1    21.205        76 ## ...        ...       ...       ... ## 1286         1   198.649       712 ## 1287         1   198.928       713 ## 1288         1   199.207       714 ## 1289         1   199.486       715 ## 1290         1   199.765       716 ##  ... 25 more variables/columns. ##  ## file(s): ## 5e1225b6b7_7859 ## 5e149b7c978_7860 ## Lazy evaluation queue: 1 processing step(s) ## Processing: ##  Filter: select retention time [20..200] on MS level(s)  [Thu Jun 25 13:55:13 2026] ##  Filter: select peaks with an m/z within [110, 120] [Thu Jun 25 13:55:13 2026] library(alabaster.base) be <- readObject(file.path(d, "backend")) be ## MsBackendMzR with 1290 spectra ##        msLevel     rtime scanIndex ##      <integer> <numeric> <integer> ## 1            1    20.089        72 ## 2            1    20.368        73 ## 3            1    20.647        74 ## 4            1    20.926        75 ## 5            1    21.205        76 ## ...        ...       ...       ... ## 1286         1   198.649       712 ## 1287         1   198.928       713 ## 1288         1   199.207       714 ## 1289         1   199.486       715 ## 1290         1   199.765       716 ##  ... 25 more variables/columns. ##  ## file(s): ## 5e1225b6b7_7859 ## 5e149b7c978_7860 be <- readMsObject(MsBackendMzR(), AlabasterParam(file.path(d, "backend"))) be ## MsBackendMzR with 1290 spectra ##        msLevel     rtime scanIndex ##      <integer> <numeric> <integer> ## 1            1    20.089        72 ## 2            1    20.368        73 ## 3            1    20.647        74 ## 4            1    20.926        75 ## 5            1    21.205        76 ## ...        ...       ...       ... ## 1286         1   198.649       712 ## 1287         1   198.928       713 ## 1288         1   199.207       714 ## 1289         1   199.486       715 ## 1290         1   199.765       716 ##  ... 25 more variables/columns. ##  ## file(s): ## 5e1225b6b7_7859 ## 5e149b7c978_7860

title: Savely Store MS Data Objects in a Portable Stash
text: example Spectra object uses MsBackendMzR backend keeps limited information memory retrieves peaks data (.e., m/z intensity values) original MS data files upon demand. stash MsBackendMzR objects contains therefore also spectra metadata reference original MS data files - peaks data. original MS data files moved different location SpectraStash folder moved another computer, updated path raw MS data files need provided spectraPath parameter readMsObject() function. alternative, also possible create self-contained stash setting consolidate = TRUE saveMsObject(). save Spectra object , time self-contained stash. consolidate = TRUE parameter passed saveMsObject() call MsBackend, , MsBackendMzR copies original MS data files stash folder: Note two additional files backend folder - original MS data files mzML format. self-contained stash folder allows restore full data even stash moved another file system. course, depending size data set respective raw MS data files, stash folder can become large.
code: d2 <- file.path(tempdir(), "spectra_stash2") saveMsObject(sps, AlabasterParam(d2), consolidate = TRUE) dir_tree(d2) ## /tmp/RtmpYSFV3h/spectra_stash2 ## ├── OBJECT ## ├── _environment.json ## ├── backend ## │   ├── 5e1225b6b7_7859 ## │   ├── 5e149b7c978_7860 ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   └── spectra_data ## │       ├── OBJECT ## │       └── basic_columns.h5 ## ├── metadata ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   └── list_contents.json.gz ## ├── processing ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   └── contents.h5 ## ├── processing_chunk_size ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   └── contents.h5 ## ├── processing_queue_variables ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   └── contents.h5 ## └── spectra_processing_queue.json

what: Stashes for Spectra with in-memory backends
title: Savely Store MS Data Objects in a Portable Stash
text: addition -disk backends MsBackendMzR MsBackendHdf5Peaks, Spectra defines also -memory backends MsBackendMemory MsBackendDataFrame, keep full MS data memory. change backend sps object MsBackendMemory: next stash updated Spectra object removing first stash directory previous SpectraStash (overwriting stash directories allowed). Inspecting content stash folder can see different structure: MS peaks data now stored within file peaks.h5, file HDF5 format used MsBackendHdf5Peaks backend: saving -memory backends changes data first MsBackendHdf5Peaks backend stored additional backend sub-folder stash. can restore Spectra object : addition, can restore MsBackendMemory : also MsBackendHdf5Peaks used actual data storage format -memory MsBackendMemory (note double backend sub-folder):
code: sps <- setBackend(sps, MsBackendMemory()) sps ## MSn data (Spectra) with 1290 spectra in a MsBackendMemory backend: ##        msLevel     rtime scanIndex ##      <integer> <numeric> <integer> ## 1            1    20.089        72 ## 2            1    20.368        73 ## 3            1    20.647        74 ## 4            1    20.926        75 ## 5            1    21.205        76 ## ...        ...       ...       ... ## 1286         1   198.649       712 ## 1287         1   198.928       713 ## 1288         1   199.207       714 ## 1289         1   199.486       715 ## 1290         1   199.765       716 ##  ... 34 more variables/columns. ## Lazy evaluation queue: 1 processing step(s) ## Processing: ##  Filter: select retention time [20..200] on MS level(s)  [Thu Jun 25 13:55:13 2026] ##  Filter: select peaks with an m/z within [110, 120] [Thu Jun 25 13:55:13 2026] ##  Switch backend from MsBackendMzR to MsBackendMemory [Thu Jun 25 13:55:14 2026] #' Remove the existing SepctraStash unlink(d2, recursive = TRUE)  #' Store the `Spectra` object in alabaster format saveMsObject(sps, AlabasterParam(d2)) dir_tree(d2) ## /tmp/RtmpYSFV3h/spectra_stash2 ## ├── OBJECT ## ├── _environment.json ## ├── backend ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   └── backend ## │       ├── OBJECT ## │       ├── mod_count ## │       │   ├── OBJECT ## │       │   └── contents.h5 ## │       ├── peaks.h5 ## │       └── spectra_data ## │           ├── OBJECT ## │           └── basic_columns.h5 ## ├── metadata ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   └── list_contents.json.gz ## ├── processing ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   └── contents.h5 ## ├── processing_chunk_size ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   └── contents.h5 ## ├── processing_queue_variables ## │   ├── OBJECT ## │   └── contents.h5 ## └── spectra_processing_queue.json readMsObject(Spectra(), AlabasterParam(d2)) ## MSn data (Spectra) with 1290 spectra in a MsBackendMemory backend: ##        msLevel     rtime scanIndex ##      <integer> <numeric> <integer> ## 1            1    20.089         1 ## 2            1    20.368         2 ## 3            1    20.647         3 ## 4            1    20.926         4 ## 5            1    21.205         5 ## ...        ...       ...       ... ## 1286         1   198.649      1286 ## 1287         1   198.928      1287 ## 1288         1   199.207      1288 ## 1289         1   199.486      1289 ## 1290         1   199.765      1290 ##  ... 25 more variables/columns. ## Lazy evaluation queue: 1 processing step(s) ## Processing: ##  Filter: select retention time [20..200] on MS level(s)  [Thu Jun 25 13:55:13 2026] ##  Filter: select peaks with an m/z within [110, 120] [Thu Jun 25 13:55:13 2026] ##  Switch backend from MsBackendMzR to MsBackendMemory [Thu Jun 25 13:55:14 2026] readMsObject(MsBackendMemory(), AlabasterParam(file.path(d2, "backend"))) ## MsBackendMemory with 1290 spectra ##        msLevel     rtime scanIndex ##      <integer> <numeric> <integer> ## 1            1    20.089         1 ## 2            1    20.368         2 ## 3            1    20.647         3 ## 4            1    20.926         4 ## 5            1    21.205         5 ## ...        ...       ...       ... ## 1286         1   198.649      1286 ## 1287         1   198.928      1287 ## 1288         1   199.207      1288 ## 1289         1   199.486      1289 ## 1290         1   199.765      1290 ##  ... 25 more variables/columns. readMsObject(MsBackendHdf5Peaks(),              AlabasterParam(file.path(d2, "backend", "backend"))) ## MsBackendHdf5Peaks with 1290 spectra ##        msLevel     rtime scanIndex ##      <integer> <numeric> <integer> ## 1            1    20.089         1 ## 2            1    20.368         2 ## 3            1    20.647         3 ## 4            1    20.926         4 ## 5            1    21.205         5 ## ...        ...       ...       ... ## 1286         1   198.649      1286 ## 1287         1   198.928      1287 ## 1288         1   199.207      1288 ## 1289         1   199.486      1289 ## 1290         1   199.765      1290 ##  ... 25 more variables/columns. ##  ## file(s): ##  peaks.h5

text: safe way store Spectra objects interoperable formats.

title: Serialize and restore Spectra objects in interoperable file formats
text: MsStash defines framework flexible, language-agnostic import export formats mass spectrometry (MS) data objects R. SpectraStash package implements framework Spectra objects data backends.

title: Serialize and restore Spectra objects in interoperable file formats
text: 📦 Export/import Spectra objects related MsBackend implementations across interoperable file formats 🧩 Modular design via S4 parameter classes generic methods 🔄 Fully integrated Bioconductor’s alabaster.base package.

title: Serialize and restore Spectra objects in interoperable file formats

title: Stash for MsBackendCached — MsBackendCachedStash
text: Spectra::MsBackendCached backend keeps cache spectra metadata (spectra variables) internal data.frame adding thus support (temporarily) changing adding spectra variables purely read-MsBackend classes. backend used e.g. MsBackendSql::MsBackendSql backend allowing add replace spectra variables without affecting content underlying data base used MsBackendSql. stash MsBackendCached contains therefore local spectra data cache (present), names available spectra variables total number spectra. Supported stash formats listed sections .

title: Stash for MsBackendCached — MsBackendCachedStash
code: # S4 method for class 'MsBackendCached,PlainTextParam' saveMsObject(object, param, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendCached,PlainTextParam' readMsObject(object, param, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendCached' saveObject(x, path, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendCached,AlabasterParam' saveMsObject(object, param, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendCached,AlabasterParam' readMsObject(object, param, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendInMemory,AlabasterParam' readMsObject(object, param, ...)

title: Stash for MsBackendCached — MsBackendCachedStash
text: object MsBackendCached object. param Either PlainTextParam AlabasterParam. ... Currently ignored. x MsBackendCached object. path saveObject(): character(1) path object stored .

title: Stash for MsBackendCached — MsBackendCachedStash
text: readMsObject() returns Spectra::MsBackendCached object.

title: Stash for MsBackendCached — MsBackendCachedStash
text: Notes stash-functionality MsBackend objects extending MsBackendCached: saveMsObject() saveObject() fail stash directory already exist. Thus, stash functions backend implementations extending MsBackendCached first call MsBackendCached's saveMsObject() saveObject() exporting respective content stash directory.

title: Stash for MsBackendCached — MsBackendCachedStash
text: data files written stash : ms_backend_data.txt: tabulator separated text file content @localData slot. ms_backend_nspectra.txt: number spectra. ms_backend_spectra_variables.txt: names object's spectra variables (tabulator separated).

title: Stash for MsBackendCached — MsBackendCachedStash
text: content slots MsBackendCached stored using functionality alabaster.base package separate sub-folders base stash directory. : local_data: data.frame locally cached spectra variables (slot @localData). nspectra: (integer(1)) number spectra. spectra_variables: (character) names object's spectra variables.

title: Stash for MsBackendCached — MsBackendCachedStash
text: Johannes Rainer

title: Stash for MsBackendCached — MsBackendCachedStash
code: library(Spectra) #> Loading required package: S4Vectors #> Loading required package: stats4 #> Loading required package: BiocGenerics #> Loading required package: generics #>  #> Attaching package: ‘generics’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     as.difftime, as.factor, as.ordered, intersect, is.element, setdiff, #>     setequal, union #>  #> Attaching package: ‘BiocGenerics’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:stats’: #>  #>     IQR, mad, sd, var, xtabs #> The following object is masked from ‘package:utils’: #>  #>     data #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     Filter, Find, Map, Position, Reduce, anyDuplicated, aperm, append, #>     as.data.frame, basename, cbind, colnames, dirname, do.call, #>     duplicated, eval, evalq, get, grep, grepl, is.unsorted, lapply, #>     mapply, match, mget, order, paste, pmax, pmax.int, pmin, pmin.int, #>     rank, rbind, rownames, sapply, saveRDS, scale, sequence, table, #>     tapply, transform, unique, unsplit, which.max, which.min #>  #> Attaching package: ‘S4Vectors’ #> The following object is masked from ‘package:utils’: #>  #>     findMatches #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     I, expand.grid, unname #> Loading required package: BiocParallel  ## Create an empty `MsBackendCached` object be <- MsBackendCached()  ## Stash the object in alabaster-format in a temporary directory ap <- AlabasterParam(file.path(tempdir(), "cache-stash")) saveMsObject(be, ap)  ## The content of the stash folder: dir(file.path(tempdir(), "cache-stash")) #> [1] "OBJECT"            "_environment.json" "local_data"        #> [4] "nspectra"          "spectra_variables"  ## Restore the object res <- readMsObject(MsBackendCached(), ap) res #> MsBackendCached with 0 spectra

title: MsBackendHdf5Peaks Stash — MsBackendHdf5PeaksStash
text: MsBackendHdf5Peaks classes can stashed (read ) plain text file-based alabaster-based formats using saveMsObject() readMsObject() functions combined PlainTextParam AlabasterParam parameter objects, respectively. cases, data files stored specified directory. default, backend's spectra metadata stored folder. Setting parameter consolidate = TRUE also copy HDF5-format peaks data files (containing m/z intensity values) backend folder generating self-consistent stash. paths data storage files also updated relative paths enabling directly restore object stash stash folder copied another computer location file system (.e., without use parameter spectraPath). Details stored files provided sections .

title: MsBackendHdf5Peaks Stash — MsBackendHdf5PeaksStash
code: # S4 method for class 'MsBackendHdf5Peaks,PlainTextParam' saveMsObject(object, param, consolidate = FALSE)  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendHdf5Peaks,PlainTextParam' readMsObject(object, param, spectraPath = character())  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendHdf5Peaks' saveObject(x, path, consolidate = FALSE, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendHdf5Peaks,AlabasterParam' saveMsObject(object, param, consolidate = FALSE)  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendHdf5Peaks,AlabasterParam' readMsObject(object, param, spectraPath = character())

title: MsBackendHdf5Peaks Stash — MsBackendHdf5PeaksStash
text: object MsBackendHdf5Peaks object. param Either PlainTextParam AlabasterParam. consolidate logical(1) whether addition spectra metadata also peaks data file (HDF5 format) stored stash folder. Default consolidate = FALSE. spectraPath readMsObject(): optional character(1) path peaks data HDF5-format (case longer available folder referred original stashed MsBackendHdf5Peaks object). x MsBackendHdf5Peaks object. path saveObject(): character(1) path object stored . ... Currently ignored.

title: MsBackendHdf5Peaks Stash — MsBackendHdf5PeaksStash
text: readMsObject() returns Spectra::MsBackendHdf5Peaks` object.

title: MsBackendHdf5Peaks Stash — MsBackendHdf5PeaksStash
text: MsBackendHdf5Peaks stash default contain spectra metadata (spectra variables) m/z intensity values. reference original HDF5-format peaks data files stored spectra variable dataStorage. files moved stash copied different computer, path original data files needs provided readMsObject() function parameter spectraPath. Alternatively, consolidate = TRUE, also possible copy peaks data files stash directory generating self-contained data stash. Note however case two copies data files exist (original location stash directory).

title: MsBackendHdf5Peaks Stash — MsBackendHdf5PeaksStash
text: saveMsObject() function PlainTextParam stores spectra metadata (spectra variables) MsBackendHdf5Peaks plain tabulator delimited text file name ms_backend_spectra_data.txt directory specified parameter path PlainTextParam object. Depending parameter consolidate also peaks data files (HDF5 format) copied stash folder (consolidate = TRUE).

title: MsBackendHdf5Peaks Stash — MsBackendHdf5PeaksStash
text: AlabasterParam, spectra metadata exported (imported) alabaster framework. Similar PlainTextParam, consolidate = TRUE also copy HDF5-format peaks data files stash directory.

title: MsBackendHdf5Peaks Stash — MsBackendHdf5PeaksStash
text: Johannes Rainer

title: MsBackendHdf5Peaks Stash — MsBackendHdf5PeaksStash
code: library(Spectra) library(SpectraStash) ## Create an example MsBackendHdf5Peaks backend from a single mzML file library(MsDataHub) tmp <- backendInitialize(MsBackendMzR(), X20171016_POOL_POS_1_105.134.mzML()) #> see ?MsDataHub and browseVignettes('MsDataHub') for documentation #> loading from cache be_h5 <- backendInitialize(MsBackendHdf5Peaks(), data = spectraData(tmp),     hdf5path = file.path(tempdir(), "h5_backend")) be_h5 #> MsBackendHdf5Peaks with 931 spectra #>       msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>     <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1           1     0.280         1 #> 2           1     0.559         2 #> 3           1     0.838         3 #> 4           1     1.117         4 #> 5           1     1.396         5 #> ...       ...       ...       ... #> 927         1   258.641       927 #> 928         1   258.920       928 #> 929         1   259.199       929 #> 930         1   259.478       930 #> 931         1   259.757       931 #>  ... 34 more variables/columns. #>  #> file(s): #>  5e1225b6b7_7859.h5  d <- file.path(tempdir(), "example_hdf5") ptp <- PlainTextParam(path = d)  ## Store the object into the stash, including the peaks data files. saveMsObject(be_h5, ptp, consolidate = TRUE)  ## List the content of the folder: ms_backend_spectra_data.txt file ## with the spectra metadata and an HDF5 file with the peaks data: dir(d) #> [1] "5e1225b6b7_7859.h5"          "ms_backend_spectra_data.txt"  ## Restore the stashed object res <- readMsObject(MsBackendHdf5Peaks(), ptp)  ## Store the object in an alabaster-format stash d <- file.path(tempdir(), "example_hdf5_2")  ap <- AlabasterParam(d) saveMsObject(be_h5, ap)  ## Check the content of the stash; with the default (`consolidate = FALSE`) ## no HDF5 data file was moved. dir(d) #> [1] "OBJECT"            "_environment.json" "mod_count"         #> [4] "spectra_data"       ## Restore the object again res <- readMsObject(MsBackendHdf5Peaks(), ap) res #> MsBackendHdf5Peaks with 931 spectra #>       msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>     <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1           1     0.280         1 #> 2           1     0.559         2 #> 3           1     0.838         3 #> 4           1     1.117         4 #> 5           1     1.396         5 #> ...       ...       ...       ... #> 927         1   258.641       927 #> 928         1   258.920       928 #> 929         1   259.199       929 #> 930         1   259.478       930 #> 931         1   259.757       931 #>  ... 25 more variables/columns. #>  #> file(s): #>  5e1225b6b7_7859.h5

what: Stash for MsBackendMemory and MsBackendDataFrame — MsBackendMemoryStash
title: Stash for MsBackendMemory and MsBackendDataFrame — MsBackendMemoryStash
text: stash -memory MS backends Spectra::MsBackendMemory Spectra::MsBackendDataFrame contains spectra variables (spectra metadata) well peaks data (m/z intensity values). Backend objects can stashed restored saveMsObject() readMsObject(), respectively. addition, saveObject() readObject() functions alabaster.base supported.

title: Stash for MsBackendMemory and MsBackendDataFrame — MsBackendMemoryStash
code: # S4 method for class 'MsBackendInMemory,PlainTextParam' saveMsObject(object, param, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendInMemory,PlainTextParam' readMsObject(object, param, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendInMemory' saveObject(x, path, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendInMemory,AlabasterParam' saveMsObject(object, param, ...)

title: Stash for MsBackendMemory and MsBackendDataFrame — MsBackendMemoryStash
text: object MsBackendMemory MsBackendDataFrame object. param Either PlainTextParam AlabasterParam. ... Currently ignored. x MsBackendMemory MsBackendDataFrame object. path saveObject(): character(1) path object stored .

title: Stash for MsBackendMemory and MsBackendDataFrame — MsBackendMemoryStash
text: readMsObject() returns restored backend.

title: Stash for MsBackendMemory and MsBackendDataFrame — MsBackendMemoryStash
text: support storing spectra peaks variables, MsBackendMemory converted Spectra::MsBackendHdf5Peaks object full peaks data stored peaks.h5 HDF5 file within stash directory. Due nested directory structure possible load MsBackendMemory stash either MsBackendMemory MsBackendHdf5Peaks.

title: Stash for MsBackendMemory and MsBackendDataFrame — MsBackendMemoryStash
text: Currently peaks variables "mz" "intensity" supported.

title: Stash for MsBackendMemory and MsBackendDataFrame — MsBackendMemoryStash
text: MsBackendMemory stash contains text-file format MsBackendHdf5PeaksStash within sub-folder backend. loading, data first imported MsBackendHdf5Peaks converted resulting MsBackendMemory (data materialized memory).

title: Stash for MsBackendMemory and MsBackendDataFrame — MsBackendMemoryStash
text: full data stored alabaster-format MsBackendHdf5PeaksStash directory backend within stash folder. Due nesting possible load data either MsBackendMemory MsBackendHdf5Peaks.

title: Stash for MsBackendMemory and MsBackendDataFrame — MsBackendMemoryStash
text: Johannes Rainer

title: Stash for MsBackendMemory and MsBackendDataFrame — MsBackendMemoryStash
code: library(Spectra)  ## Create an example `MsBackendMemory` object. d <- data.frame(msLevel = c(1L, 2L, 1L, 1L),     rtime = c(12.1, 12.2, 13.1, 13.4)) d$mz <- list(     c(14.4, 155.2, 186.4),     c(144.3, 231.3, 345.3, 453.1),     c(111.2, 142.4, 143.1),     c(143.3, 144.3, 153.3, 532.3, 641.5) ) d$intensity <- list(     c(323.2, 53.2, 35.5),     c(43.3, 54.1, 33.1, 53.1),     c(435.3, 35312.3, 5432.5),     c(433.5, 55434.2, 43.4, 54362.1, 24435.3) ) be <- backendInitialize(MsBackendMemory(), data = d) be #> MsBackendMemory with 4 spectra #>     msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>   <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1         1      12.1        NA #> 2         2      12.2        NA #> 3         1      13.1        NA #> 4         1      13.4        NA #>  ... 16 more variables/columns.  ## Stash the object in alabaster-format to a temporary folder p <- AlabasterParam(file.path(tempdir(), "mem_stash")) saveMsObject(be, p)  ## Show directory content of the stash dir(file.path(tempdir(), "mem_stash")) #> [1] "OBJECT"            "_environment.json" "backend"            ## Restore the object from the stash res <- readMsObject(MsBackendMemory(), p) res #> MsBackendMemory with 4 spectra #>     msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>   <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1         1      12.1         1 #> 2         2      12.2         2 #> 3         1      13.1         3 #> 4         1      13.4         4 #>  ... 16 more variables/columns.  ## The in-memory backends store their full MS data through an ## [Spectra::MsBackendHdf5Peaks] backend: their peaks data is stored as a ## (single) HDF5 file within the stash. A `MsBackendMemory` stash contains ## therefore a complete stash of a `MsBackendHdf5Peaks`, which allows to ## read the data also as a `MsBackendHdf5Peaks` backend: res <- readMsObject(MsBackendHdf5Peaks(),     AlabasterParam(file.path(tempdir(), "mem_stash", "backend"))) res #> MsBackendHdf5Peaks with 4 spectra #>     msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>   <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1         1      12.1         1 #> 2         2      12.2         2 #> 3         1      13.1         3 #> 4         1      13.4         4 #>  ... 16 more variables/columns. #>  #> file(s): #>  peaks.h5  ## In addition to the `saveMsObject()` and `readMsObject()` functions, also ## the `saveObject()` and `readObject()` functions from *alabater.base* are ## supported (for stashes in alabaster format). library(alabaster.base) res <- readObject(file.path(tempdir(), "mem_stash")) res #> MsBackendMemory with 4 spectra #>     msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>   <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1         1      12.1         1 #> 2         2      12.2         2 #> 3         1      13.1         3 #> 4         1      13.4         4 #>  ... 16 more variables/columns.  ## Storing and restoring a `MsBackendDataFrame` backend or using a stash in ## plain text file-based format works analogously.

title: MsBackendMzR Stash — MsBackendMzRStash
text: MsBackendMzR classes can stashed (read ) plain text file-based alabaster-based formats using saveMsObject() readMsObject() functions combined PlainTextParam AlabasterParam parameter objects, respectively. Setting parameter consolidate = TRUE saveMsObject() saveObject() function copy also original MS data files folder generating self-consistent stash. stash folder can also copied another computer location file system without need use parameter spectraPath restoring data. Additional properties stash formats described detail sections .

title: MsBackendMzR Stash — MsBackendMzRStash
code: # S4 method for class 'MsBackendMzR,PlainTextParam' saveMsObject(object, param, consolidate = FALSE)  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendMzR,PlainTextParam' readMsObject(object, param, spectraPath = character())  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendMzR' saveObject(x, path, consolidate = FALSE, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendMzR,AlabasterParam' saveMsObject(object, param, consolidate = FALSE)  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendMzR,AlabasterParam' readMsObject(object, param, spectraPath = character())

title: MsBackendMzR Stash — MsBackendMzRStash
text: object MsBackendMzR object. param Either PlainTextParam AlabasterParam. consolidate logical(1) whether addition spectra metadata also original MS data files stored stash folder. Default consolidate = FALSE. spectraPath readMsObject(): optional character(1) path MS data files (mzML, mzXML CDF) case longer available folder referred original stashed MsBackendMzR object. x MsBackendMzR object. path saveObject(): character(1) path object stored . ... Currently ignored.

title: MsBackendMzR Stash — MsBackendMzRStash
text: readMsObject() returns Spectra::MsBackendMzR` object.

title: MsBackendMzR Stash — MsBackendMzRStash
text: MsBackendMzR objects contain peaks data (.e., m/z intensity values) retrieve original MS data files (mzML, mzXML CDF format). Unless consolidate = TRUE used, MsBackendMzR stash therefore contain spectra metadata (.e., spectra variables) peaks data. reference original MS data files stored spectra variable dataStorage files longer available directory specified dataStorage restored object valid, unless new location provided parameter spectraPath.

title: MsBackendMzR Stash — MsBackendMzRStash
text: saveMsObject() function PlainTextParam stores spectra metadata (spectra variables) MsBackendMzR plain tabulator delimited text file name ms_backend_spectra_data.txt directory specified parameter path PlainTextParam object. Importantly, peaks data (m/z intensity values) exported saveMsObject(). readMsObject() restores previously stashed MsBackendMzR object directory specified parameter path PlainTextParam. additional parameter spectraPath readMsObject() allows define path MS data files containing full MS data (.e., mzML, mzXML CDF files referred MsBackendMzR).

title: MsBackendMzR Stash — MsBackendMzRStash
text: saveMsObject() AlabasterParam parameter object stashes provided MsBackendMzR object alabster-based format directory defined argument param AlabasterParam. readMsObject() AlabasterParam restores previously stashed MsBackend object. Optional parameter spectraPath allows specify storage path MS data files referenced MsBackendMzR (case longer directory saving object). addition, alabaster methods saveObject() readObject() can used save read MsBackendMzR objects.

title: MsBackendMzR Stash — MsBackendMzRStash
text: Philippine Louail, Johannes Rainer

title: MsBackendMzR Stash — MsBackendMzRStash
code: library(SpectraStash) library(Spectra) library(MsDataHub)  ## Create a MsBackendMzR from test data be <- backendInitialize(MsBackendMzR(), PestMix1_DDA.mzML()) #> see ?MsDataHub and browseVignettes('MsDataHub') for documentation #> loading from cache be #> MsBackendMzR with 7602 spectra #>        msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>      <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1            1     0.231         1 #> 2            1     0.351         2 #> 3            1     0.471         3 #> 4            1     0.591         4 #> 5            1     0.711         5 #> ...        ...       ...       ... #> 7598         1   899.491      7598 #> 7599         1   899.613      7599 #> 7600         1   899.747      7600 #> 7601         1   899.872      7601 #> 7602         1   899.993      7602 #>  ... 34 more variables/columns. #>  #> file(s): #> 5e12888a090_7861  ## Define a folder where to stash the object pth <- file.path(tempdir(), "mzr_stash")  ## Stash the object to this folder in a plain text-based format saveMsObject(be, PlainTextParam(pth))  ## Restore the stashed object res <- readMsObject(MsBackendMzR(), PlainTextParam(pth)) res #> MsBackendMzR with 7602 spectra #>        msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>      <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1            1     0.231         1 #> 2            1     0.351         2 #> 3            1     0.471         3 #> 4            1     0.591         4 #> 5            1     0.711         5 #> ...        ...       ...       ... #> 7598         1   899.491      7598 #> 7599         1   899.613      7599 #> 7600         1   899.747      7600 #> 7601         1   899.872      7601 #> 7602         1   899.993      7602 #>  ... 27 more variables/columns. #>  #> file(s): #> 5e12888a090_7861  ## Clean-up unlink(pth, recursive = TRUE)  ## Store the data in *alabaster* format including also the original MS ## data files (`consolidate = TRUE`) saveMsObject(be, AlabasterParam(pth), consolidate = TRUE)  ## Get the directory content of the stash folder: dir(pth) #> [1] "5e12888a090_7861"  "OBJECT"            "_environment.json" #> [4] "spectra_data"       ## Restore the object res <- readMsObject(MsBackendMzR(), AlabasterParam(pth)) res #> MsBackendMzR with 7602 spectra #>        msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>      <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1            1     0.231         1 #> 2            1     0.351         2 #> 3            1     0.471         3 #> 4            1     0.591         4 #> 5            1     0.711         5 #> ...        ...       ...       ... #> 7598         1   899.491      7598 #> 7599         1   899.613      7599 #> 7600         1   899.747      7600 #> 7601         1   899.872      7601 #> 7602         1   899.993      7602 #>  ... 27 more variables/columns. #>  #> file(s): #> 5e12888a090_7861  ## If the data is exported with `consolidate = FALSE` (the default), the ## new location of MS data files could be provided with parameter ## `spectraPath` of the `readMsObject()` function in case they are no ## longer in the path referenced by the stashed object.  ## Clean-up unlink(pth, recursive = TRUE)

title: Spectra Stash — SpectraStash
text: Spectra objects can stashed (read ) plain text file-based alabaster-based formats using saveMsObject() readMsObject() functions configured PlainTextParam AlabasterParam parameter objects, respectively. cases, data Spectra object stored stash directory defined parameter object. Depending used MS backend (parameters used), stash can also contain MS data files. present, Spectra objects using one following MS data backends supported: MsBackendMzR: see MsBackendMzRStash details options. MsBackendHdf5Peaks: see MsBackendHdf5PeaksStash details options. data backend Spectra backend can eventually changed one backends using Spectra::setBackend() method support saving object Spectra stash. Support additional backends respectively data representations might also provided separate R packages. Details stash formats provided respective sections .

title: Spectra Stash — SpectraStash
code: # S4 method for class 'Spectra,PlainTextParam' saveMsObject(object, param, ...)  # S4 method for class 'Spectra,PlainTextParam' readMsObject(object, param, ...)  # S4 method for class 'Spectra' saveObject(x, path, ...)  # S4 method for class 'Spectra,AlabasterParam' saveMsObject(object, param, ...)  # S4 method for class 'Spectra,AlabasterParam' readMsObject(object, param, ...)

title: Spectra Stash — SpectraStash
text: object Spectra object. param Either PlainTextParam AlabasterParam. ... additional arguments passed saveMsObject readMsObject method Spectra's MsBackend, example consolidate spectraPath. See saveMsObject() readMsObject() documentation used MsBackend class information supported arguments. x Spectra object. path saveObject(): character(1) path object stored .

title: Spectra Stash — SpectraStash
text: readMsObject() returns Spectra::Spectra object.

title: Spectra Stash — SpectraStash
text: format, data content Spectra object stored files: spectra_slots.txt: plain text file containning processing queue variables (separated "|"), processing log messages (separated "|"), processing chunk size MsBackend class used. spectra_processing_queue.json: object's processing queue serialized JSON format. can unserialized using jsonlite::unserializeJSON(). information MS backend's data see respective documentation.

title: Spectra Stash — SpectraStash
text: AlabasterParam, Spectra object exported (imported) alabaster framework set JSON /HDF5 files. content slot stored separate file name matching slot name (converted snake_case). object's MsBackend stored sub-folder backend within stash folder. information MS backend's data stash see respective documentation.

title: Spectra Stash — SpectraStash
text: Johannes Rainer, Philippine Louail

title: Spectra Stash — SpectraStash
code: ## Create a Spectra object from two example MS data files (from MsDataHub) library(Spectra) library(MsDataHub) s <- Spectra(     c(X20171016_POOL_POS_1_105.134.mzML(),       X20171016_POOL_POS_3_105.134.mzML())) #> see ?MsDataHub and browseVignettes('MsDataHub') for documentation #> loading from cache #> see ?MsDataHub and browseVignettes('MsDataHub') for documentation #> loading from cache s #> MSn data (Spectra) with 1862 spectra in a MsBackendMzR backend: #>        msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>      <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1            1     0.280         1 #> 2            1     0.559         2 #> 3            1     0.838         3 #> 4            1     1.117         4 #> 5            1     1.396         5 #> ...        ...       ...       ... #> 1858         1   258.636       927 #> 1859         1   258.915       928 #> 1860         1   259.194       929 #> 1861         1   259.473       930 #> 1862         1   259.752       931 #>  ... 34 more variables/columns. #>  #> file(s): #> 5e1225b6b7_7859 #> 5e149b7c978_7860  ## Filter the intensities of the Spectra removing peaks with an intensity ## below 100 s <- filterIntensity(s, intensity = 100)  ## Define the format and location of the `Spectra` stash: use the ## *alabaster*-based format and store the stash in a folder named ## *spectra_stash* in a temporary directory ap <- AlabasterParam(file.path(tempdir(), "spectra_stash"))  ## Stash the `Spectra` object copying in addition the MS data files into the ## stash (`consolidate = TRUE`). saveMsObject(s, ap, consolidate = TRUE)  ## Show the content of the stash dir(ap@path) #> [1] "OBJECT"                        "_environment.json"             #> [3] "backend"                       "metadata"                      #> [5] "processing"                    "processing_chunk_size"         #> [7] "processing_queue_variables"    "spectra_processing_queue.json"  ## Read the `Spectra` object from the stash: res <- readMsObject(Spectra(), ap) res #> MSn data (Spectra) with 1862 spectra in a MsBackendMzR backend: #>        msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>      <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1            1     0.280         1 #> 2            1     0.559         2 #> 3            1     0.838         3 #> 4            1     1.117         4 #> 5            1     1.396         5 #> ...        ...       ...       ... #> 1858         1   258.636       927 #> 1859         1   258.915       928 #> 1860         1   259.194       929 #> 1861         1   259.473       930 #> 1862         1   259.752       931 #>  ... 25 more variables/columns. #>  #> file(s): #> 5e1225b6b7_7859 #> 5e149b7c978_7860 #> Lazy evaluation queue: 1 processing step(s) #> Processing: #>  Remove peaks with intensities outside [100, Inf] in spectra of MS level(s) 1. [Thu Jun 25 13:55:03 2026]   ## It is also possible to read individual contents from the stash. The ## directory *backend* contains for example the stashed `MsBackend` of the ## `Spectra` object. To read only the `MsBackend`: ap2 <- AlabasterParam(file.path(tempdir(), "spectra_stash", "backend")) b <- readMsObject(MsBackendMzR(), ap2) b #> MsBackendMzR with 1862 spectra #>        msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>      <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1            1     0.280         1 #> 2            1     0.559         2 #> 3            1     0.838         3 #> 4            1     1.117         4 #> 5            1     1.396         5 #> ...        ...       ...       ... #> 1858         1   258.636       927 #> 1859         1   258.915       928 #> 1860         1   259.194       929 #> 1861         1   259.473       930 #> 1862         1   259.752       931 #>  ... 25 more variables/columns. #>  #> file(s): #> 5e1225b6b7_7859 #> 5e149b7c978_7860  ## Alternatively, that data can also be read directly with the `readObject()` ## method from the *alabaster.base* package: library(alabaster.base) b <- readObject(file.path(ap@path, "backend")) b #> MsBackendMzR with 1862 spectra #>        msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>      <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1            1     0.280         1 #> 2            1     0.559         2 #> 3            1     0.838         3 #> 4            1     1.117         4 #> 5            1     1.396         5 #> ...        ...       ...       ... #> 1858         1   258.636       927 #> 1859         1   258.915       928 #> 1860         1   259.194       929 #> 1861         1   259.473       930 #> 1862         1   259.752       931 #>  ... 25 more variables/columns. #>  #> file(s): #> 5e1225b6b7_7859 #> 5e149b7c978_7860
